summary_one_line: Input must drive compose_hebrew to found==true (or set has_gpos_mark) so line 81 takes its false branch instead of the Hebrew fallback.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A font/buffer payload where the base Unicode composition c->unicode->compose(a,b,ab) succeeds (found==true) or has_gpos_mark is set, so the conjunction !found && !has_gpos_mark is false. Winning seeds carry BCP47-tag/structural bytes (e.g. 'zh-hant-ht', 'zh-H[nt-ht') that steer the shaper into a font path producing successful composition rather than the Hebrew presentation-form fallback.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds reach line 81 and take BOTH sides (T=14 F=12), i.e. they do reach the false branch and resolve it; avg hitcount on branch winner=8 vs loser=0. They are diverse naive/value_profile havoc/structural inputs (ASCII language-tag runs) that occasionally make found==true so the fallback is skipped.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds hit line 81 only on the true side (T=67 F=0) and never the false side; their bytes are Hebrew-combining-mark-like sequences that miss compose() and keep re-entering the switch(b) fallback. The I2S fuzzers are stuck pumping CMP-matched combining-mark codepoints into the switch arms and never escape to found==true.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The WINNERS are the non-I2S fuzzers (naive beats cmplog; value_profile beats value_profile_cmplog). The escape to the false branch is NOT a CMP-solving event: it needs the broad structural/havoc exploration of the input that occasionally yields a successfully-composing base+mark pair. The I2S/CMP_MAP machinery actively over-fits the loser onto the switch(b) equality arms (matching Hebrew mark literals like 0x05B7/0x05BC), which is exactly the true-branch trap, so the I2S fuzzers blocked. Winner = plain havoc/structural mutation, with no CMP-operand substitution involved (havoc-only).

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false branch of the compose_hebrew guard at line 81.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
Source line:   if (!found && !c->plan->has_gpos_mark)
Globally blocked side: F  (false branch)

evidence_trail.claim: Both decisive pairs have the non-I2S fuzzer as winner over the I2S-equipped fuzzer.
The second decisive pair is value_profile beating value_profile_cmplog with the same I2S delta.
found is computed from the base Unicode composition before the fallback guard at line 81.
Winner-resolving seeds carry BCP47 language-tag ASCII bytes, signature of naive/structural inputs.
Winner seeds reach line 81 and take its false side, while loser seeds only take the true side (blocked).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L  81  T=14 F=12  T=67 F=0  if (!found && !c->plan->has_gpos_mark)  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: Winner hits the branch (hitcount 8) while loser never does (hitcount 0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: avg hitcount on branch: winner=8  loser=0

evidence_trail.claim: The loser-blocking seeds repeatedly enter the switch(b) fallback arms keyed on Hebrew mark codepoints.
cmplog/vpc winners would be driven by I2SRandReplace substituting CMP operands into the input; here those fuzzers are the losers, so the winner's escape is not I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile (a winner) has no I2SRandReplace, only havoc/token mutators, confirming the winning escape is plain havoc/structural exploration.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is havoc-only, not I2SRandReplace, so lineage verification is not mandatory. The decisive winners are naive and value_profile, both of which lack I2SRandReplace per MECHANISM CONTEXT; the I2S-equipped fuzzers (cmplog, value_profile_cmplog) are the LOSERS. There is no winning cmplog/vpc seed whose lineage could carry an I2S-floor dash row to attribute the win to I2S. The winner's escape to the false branch (found==true) is reached by broad havoc/structural byte exploration, not CMP-operand substitution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness in which adding I2SRandReplace (cmplog) makes the fuzzer reach line 81's false branch MORE often (or as often) than naive — i.e. the I2S fuzzers ceasing to be the losers — would refute the claim that I2S actively traps the fuzzer in the switch(b) fallback and that the escape is havoc-only.

weakest_evidence_point: The causal claim that I2S/CMP_MAP feedback actively over-fits the loser onto the switch(b) equality arms (rather than the I2S fuzzers merely being slower for unrelated reasons) is inferred from the true-only hit pattern, not directly observed in lineage.
